title: Note for the neighbour
prompt: Write a short note in Swedish to a neighbour.

payload.stimulus: You will be away this weekend. Write a short note (en lapp) in Swedish to your neighbour Karin. Say that you travel on Friday, ask her to water your plants (vattna blommorna), and say that you are back on Sunday evening.
criteria: Covers all three points: travel day, the request, and the return day
Uses a polite request form (kan du, snälla)
Short sentences in the present tense, with a greeting and a name

title: Text message to a colleague
prompt: Write a short text message in Swedish.

payload.stimulus: Your bus is late. Write a short SMS in Swedish to your colleague Erik. Say that the bus is late, that you arrive at nine, and say sorry.
criteria: Explains the reason for being late in simple Swedish
Gives a clear arrival time
Includes an apology (förlåt / ursäkta)

title: Filling in a form
prompt: Write short answers about yourself for a simple form.

payload.stimulus: Du ska anmäla dig till en kurs. Skriv några korta meningar om dig själv: namn, adress, telefonnummer och vilken kurs du vill gå.
criteria: Gives name, address, and a phone number
States which course the writer wants to take
Information is clear and correctly spelled

title: Your favourite food
prompt: Write about food you like in Swedish.

payload.stimulus: Skriv om mat du tycker om. Vad äter du ofta? Vem lagar maten hemma hos dig?
criteria: Names at least two dishes or food items in Swedish
Says who cooks at home
Uses present tense and simple sentence structure

title: A message to the preschool
prompt: Write a short message to your child's preschool.

payload.stimulus: Your daughter Sara cannot come to preschool tomorrow. Write a short message in Swedish. Say that she is ill, that she has a fever, and that she comes back on Monday.
criteria: States that the child is ill and gives the symptom
Says when the child will return
Includes a greeting and the parent's name

title: Your work or studies
prompt: Write about your work or your studies in Swedish.

payload.stimulus: Skriv om ditt jobb eller dina studier. Vad gör du? När börjar och slutar du? Vad tycker du om det?
criteria: Describes the job or the studies concretely
Gives at least one time (klockan åtta, på förmiddagen)
Includes one simple opinion in correct Swedish

title: Asking about an appointment
prompt: Write a short message asking to change an appointment.

payload.stimulus: You have an appointment at Försäkringskassan on Thursday, but you must work. Write a short message in Swedish. Ask for a new time and suggest Friday.
criteria: Explains why the current time does not work
Asks clearly for a new appointment and suggests a day
Polite tone with a greeting and the writer's name

title: Ask for the way
prompt: Ask for directions out loud in Swedish.

payload.stimulus: Du står i centrum i Stockholm och vill hitta stationen. Fråga en person om vägen och tacka efteråt.
criteria: Uses a polite opening (ursäkta) and a clear question
Asks about a specific place
Ends the conversation politely (tack)

title: Order in a café
prompt: Order food and drink out loud in Swedish.

payload.stimulus: Du är på ett kafé. Beställ något att dricka och något att äta. Fråga också vad det kostar.
criteria: Orders at least one drink and one food item
Asks about the price in Swedish
Uses polite phrases (jag vill ha, tack)

title: Talk about the weather
prompt: Describe the weather out loud in Swedish.

payload.stimulus: Berätta hur vädret är i dag och hur vädret är på vintern där du bor.
criteria: Describes today's weather with at least two words (kallt, regnar, blåser)
Compares with the winter weather
Speaks in clear, simple sentences

title: Call in sick to work
prompt: Make a short sick call out loud.

payload.stimulus: Ring till din chef Johan. Säg vem du är, att du är sjuk i dag och när du kommer tillbaka.
criteria: Introduces themselves at the start of the call
Says they are ill and mentions when they will return
Speech is polite and understandable

title: Your free time
prompt: Talk about your free time in Swedish.

payload.stimulus: Berätta vad du gör på fritiden. Vad tycker du om att göra? Vem gör du det med?
criteria: Names at least two free-time activities
Says who they do it with
Uses jag tycker om att + verb correctly at least once

title: Ask a colleague a question
prompt: Ask a work question out loud in Swedish.

payload.stimulus: Du är ny på jobbet. Fråga din kollega Fatima när rasten är, var du kan äta lunch och vem du ska prata med om schemat.
criteria: Asks three clear questions in Swedish
Uses correct question words (när, var, vem)
Tone is polite and the speech is easy to follow

title: Why you study Swedish
prompt: Explain why you are learning Swedish.

payload.stimulus: Berätta varför du läser svenska. Vad vill du göra i framtiden? Hur ofta studerar du?
criteria: Gives at least one clear reason for studying Swedish
Mentions a future plan in simple language
Says how often they study, using a time expression

title: Describe a photo of a family meal
prompt: Describe a simple everyday scene out loud.

payload.stimulus: Titta på bilden i huvudet: en familj äter middag i ett kök. Beskriv vad du ser. Vilka personer finns där? Vad gör de? Vad står på bordet?
criteria: Describes people and what they are doing
Names at least two objects in the room
Uses det finns / det är and present tense correctly
